title: Vibe in Patriots building reportedly better than two weeks ago with Mike Vrabel
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Mike Vrabel returned to the field for Patriots rookie minicamp, bringing familiar energy seen in previous seasons.
Vrabel's offseason was overshadowed by a scandal, but recent reports indicate a positive shift in team atmosphere.
The Patriots are focused on their 2026 season, supported by potential acquisition of wide receiver A.J. Brown.

title: Willson Contreras’s go-ahead HR, Payton Tolle’s 8 strong innings lift Red Sox past Braves 3-2
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Willson Contreras hit a decisive two-run home run in the eighth inning, securing a 3-2 win for the Red Sox.
Payton Tolle pitched a career-high eight innings, allowing two earned runs, as the Red Sox defeated the Braves.
Aroldis Chapman saved the game despite a bases-loaded situation in the ninth inning, ending the Braves' threat.

title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story placed on 10-day injured list with a sports hernia.
Nick Sogard recalled from Triple-A Worcester to replace Story on the Red Sox roster.
Story considers surgery options as sports hernia recovery could sideline him for 6-10 weeks.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old man died in Marlborough, NH, after his pickup truck collided head-on with a school bus carrying 13 students.
The crash occurred on Route 12 near Webb Depot Road; the bus driver and students were taken to the hospital with minor injuries.
The incident is under investigation by the NH State Police; witnesses are asked to contact Trooper Eric Torrens.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Mike Yastrzemski's walk-off hit in the 10th inning gave the Braves a 3-2 victory over the Red Sox.
Red Sox fall to a 18-26 record, struggling with offensive consistency and leaving seven on base against Atlanta.
Starting pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs but had a solid outing with six strikeouts over five innings.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs is interested in rejoining the Patriots, but an A.J. Brown trade complicates the situation.
Patriots face a salary cap issue with A.J. Brown's $32 million contract and Romeo Doubs' $68 million commitment.
Albert Breer suggests a Diggs return is unlikely if Brown joins, due to cap space and Diggs' role concerns.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes, a 15-year-old boy from Dorchester, was last seen Tuesday afternoon near 965 Blue Hill Avenue.
Boston Police describe LaGrant-Gomes as 5'8", thin build, with brown eyes and black hair, known to frequent various areas.
Anyone with information is encouraged to contact District B-3 detectives at 617-343-4712 or submit anonymous tips via CrimeStoppers.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, allegedly shot his girlfriend's mother and another man in Dorchester on Mother's Day.
Both victims were found with gunshot wounds outside a Kingsdale Street home, woman critically injured, man less so.
Broomes was charged with multiple offenses and held without bail after a hearing in Dorchester District Court.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Five former Red Sox pitchers have combined for a 2.74 ERA in 39 starts in 2026.
A 2026 lineup of former Red Sox includes Mookie Betts, Xander Bogaerts, and Kyle Schwarber.
Schwarber leads MLB with 18 home runs; Dominic Smith bats over .350 as of May 15.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony is making progress recovering from a right wrist sprain sustained on May 4.
Anthony removed his wrist brace after receiving a cortisone injection and participated in baseball activities like playing catch.
Despite improvements, Anthony has not resumed batting, but feels encouraged by his progress and hopes to start hitting soon.
